name: Bread Pudding with Bourbon Sauce- T/l
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Brush softened butter evenly over the bottom and sides of a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking/serving dish. Set aside.
3. Break bread into chunks and add to a bowl with milk. Let soak until all milk is absorbed, then crumble bread into small bits.
4. In a small bowl, beat eggs and sugar together with a wire whisk or electric beater until smooth and thick. Stir in raisins and vanilla extract.
5. Pour egg mixture over bread crumbs and stir until well combined.
6. Pour bread pudding into prepared dish, spreading evenly. Place dish in a large shallow roasting pan on the middle shelf of the oven. Add boiling water to pan to a depth of about 1 inch.
7. Bake for 1 hour, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.
Bourbon Sauce:
1 stick butter, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
1 cup sugar
1 egg
1/2 cup bourbon
1. Melt butter pieces in the top of a double boiler set over hot (not boiling) water.
2. In a small bowl, stir together sugar and egg.
3. Add egg mixture to melted butter. Stir for 2 to 3 minutes until sugar dissolves completely and egg is cooked but does not curdle.
4. Remove pan from heat and let sauce cool to room temperature before stirring in bourbon.
5. Serve bread pudding immediately, directly from the baking dish, with sauce served separately in a sauceboat or small bowl.
NOTE:
The better the bourbon, the better the sauce.
